notes:
VERIFIED + CORRECTED. Set-specific source = PokeBeach 12-box (2-case = 432-pack) shop-owner study. Raw counts (independently corroborated via two WebSearch snippet retrievals + Chuggaaconroy tweet): 51 regular GX, 14 Full Art (GX + FA Trainer), 11 Secret Rare (5 Item/gold + 6 Rainbow), 24 Prism Star. Mapped onto this set's COARSE buckets {common,uncommon,rare,ultra-rare,secret-rare}, one rare-or-better slot/pack:
- ultra-rare = (51 GX + 14 FA)/432 = 65/432 = 0.150463 (1 in 6.65 packs).
- secret-rare = 11/432 = 0.025463 (1 in 39.3 packs, ~0.92/box).
- rare = residual 1 - 0.150463 - 0.025463 = 0.824074. This absorbs holo + non-holo rares AND Prism Stars (TCGdex/engine has no prism bucket; a Rare Prism Star is neither ultra nor secret, so it correctly lands in 'rare'). Embedded Prism share = 24/432 = 0.0556 (1 in 18) is part of that rare mass.
CORRECTION vs draft: draft computed UR/SR by round-tripping through ROUNDED per-box averages (e.g. 0.91*12=10.92 instead of the raw 11 SR), giving slightly drifted 0.15028/0.02528. Replaced with raw-count values 0.150463/0.025463; rare residual now 0.824074. Still sums to exactly 1.0.
Total hit economy: 100 hits/432 packs = 0.23 hits/pack -> sane (NOT a GX every pack). Flipside Gaming SM baseline corroborates order of magnitude (GX ~1:9, FA ~1:18-36; combined ~1:6-7 matches 0.150; SR ~1:2 boxes, CS ran slightly hotter at ~0.92/box, consistent with documented 'hot' set).
Standard SM structure confirmed: 10 modeled cards/pack + 1 basic Energy excluded. Slots: 5 commons, 3 uncommons, 1 reverse-holo filler (modeled 50/50 common/uncommon), 1 rare-or-better slot driving EV. Products VERIFIED: official Pokemon Center 'Celestial Storm Booster Display Box (36 Booster Packs)' -> displayPacks=36; official Pokemon Center ETB listing '8 Celestial Storm booster packs' -> etbPacks=8. Bulbapedia confirms 183-card English set, 11 GX, 3 Prism Star.
